title: Food and Drugs Act

provisions.label: Short title
provisions.heading: Short Title
provisions.text: This Act may be cited as the Food and Drugs Act.

provisions.label: “advanced therapeutic product”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: advanced therapeutic product means a therapeutic product that is described in Schedule G or that belongs to a class of therapeutic products that is described in that Schedule; (produit thérapeutique innovant)

provisions.label: “advertisement”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: advertisement includes any representation by any means whatever for the purpose of promoting directly or indirectly the sale or disposal of any food, drug, cosmetic or device; (publicité ou annonce)

provisions.label: “analyst”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: analyst means an individual designated as an analyst for the purposes of this Act under section 28 or under section 13 of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act; (analyste)

provisions.label: “clinical trial”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: clinical trial means a study, involving human subjects, for the purpose of discovering or verifying the effects of a drug, a device or a food for a special dietary purpose; (essai clinique)

provisions.label: 2“confidential business information”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: that is not publicly available,

provisions.label: 2“confidential business information”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: in respect of which the person has taken measures that are reasonable in the circumstances to ensure that it remains not publicly available, and

provisions.label: 2“confidential business information”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: that has actual or potential economic value to the person or their competitors because it is not publicly available and its disclosure would result in a material financial loss to the person or a material financial gain to their competitors; (renseignements commerciaux confidentiels)

provisions.label: “confidential business information”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: confidential business information, in respect of a person to whose business or affairs the information relates, means — subject to the regulations — business information

provisions.label: “contraceptive device”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: contraceptive device means any instrument, apparatus, contrivance or substance other than a drug, that is manufactured, sold or represented for use in the prevention of conception; (moyen anticonceptionnel)

provisions.label: “cosmetic”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: cosmetic includes any substance or mixture of substances manufactured, sold or represented for use in cleansing, improving or altering the complexion, skin, hair or teeth, and includes deodorants and perfumes; (cosmétique)

provisions.label: “Department”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: Department means the Department of Health; (ministère)

provisions.label: 2“device”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: diagnosing, treating, mitigating or preventing a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or any of their symptoms, in human beings or animals,

provisions.label: 2“device”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: restoring, modifying or correcting the body structure of human beings or animals or the functioning of any part of the bodies of human beings or animals,

provisions.label: 2“device”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: diagnosing pregnancy in human beings or animals,

provisions.label: 2“device”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: caring for human beings or animals during pregnancy or at or after the birth of the offspring, including caring for the offspring, or

provisions.label: 2“device”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: preventing conception in human beings or animals;

provisions.label: “device”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: device means an instrument, apparatus, contrivance or other similar article, or an in vitro reagent, including a component, part or accessory of any of them, that is manufactured, sold or represented for use in however, it does not include such an instrument, apparatus, contrivance or article, or a component, part or accessory of any of them, that does any of the actions referred to in paragraphs (a) to (e) solely by pharmacological, immunological or metabolic means or solely by chemical means in or on the body of a human being or animal; (instrument)

provisions.label: 2“drug”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: the diagnosis, treatment, mitigation or prevention of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or its symptoms, in human beings or animals,

provisions.label: 2“drug”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: restoring, correcting or modifying organic functions in human beings or animals, or

provisions.label: 2“drug”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: disinfection in premises in which food is manufactured, prepared or kept; (drogue)

provisions.label: “drug”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: drug includes any substance or mixture of substances manufactured, sold or represented for use in

provisions.label: “food”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: food includes any article manufactured, sold or represented for use as food or drink for human beings, chewing gum, and any ingredient that may be mixed with food for any purpose whatever; (aliment)

provisions.label: 2“food for a special dietary purpose”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: to meet the particular requirements of an individual in whom a physical or physiological condition exists as a result of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or

provisions.label: 2“food for a special dietary purpose”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: to be the sole or primary source of nutrition for an individual; (aliment à des fins diététiques spéciales)

provisions.label: “food for a special dietary purpose”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: food for a special dietary purpose means a food that has been specially processed or formulated

provisions.label: “foreign regulatory authority”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: foreign regulatory authority means a government agency or other entity outside Canada that controls the manufacture, use or sale of therapeutic products or foods within its jurisdiction; (autorité réglementaire étrangère)

provisions.label: “inspector”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: inspector means an individual designated as an inspector for the purposes of this Act under subsection 22(1) or under section 13 of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act; (inspecteur)

provisions.label: “label”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: label includes any legend, word or mark attached to, included in, belonging to or accompanying any food, drug, cosmetic, device or package; (étiquette)

provisions.label: “Minister”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: Minister means the Minister of Health; (ministre)

provisions.label: “package”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: package includes any thing in which any food, drug, cosmetic or device is wholly or partly contained, placed or packed; (emballage)

provisions.label: “person”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: person means an individual or an organization as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code; (personne)

provisions.label: “prescribed”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: prescribed means prescribed by the regulations; (Version anglaise seulement)

provisions.label: 2“sell”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: offer for sale, expose for sale or have in possession for sale — or distribute to one or more persons, whether or not the distribution is made for consideration, and

provisions.label: “sell”

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: therapeutic product means a drug or device or any combination of drugs and devices; (produit thérapeutique)

provisions.label: 2“therapeutic product authorization”(a)
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: authorizes the conduct of a clinical trial in respect of a therapeutic product and is issued under the regulations, or

provisions.label: 2“therapeutic product authorization”(a)
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: authorizes, as the case may be, the import, sale, advertisement, manufacture, preparation, preservation, packaging, labelling, storage or testing of a therapeutic product and is issued under subsection 21.92(1) or the regulations, or

provisions.label: 2“therapeutic product authorization”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: an authorization, including a licence, that

provisions.label: 2“therapeutic product authorization”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: an authorization, including a licence, that would fall within paragraph (a) if the authorization were not suspended; (autorisation relative à un produit thérapeutique)

provisions.label: “therapeutic product authorization”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: therapeutic product authorization means

provisions.label: “unsanitary conditions”
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: unsanitary conditions means such conditions or circumstances as might contaminate with dirt or filth, or render injurious to health, a food, drug or cosmetic. (conditions non hygiéniques)

provisions.label: Definitions
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: In this Act,

provisions.label: Non-corrective contact lenses
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: For the purposes of this Act, a non-corrective contact lens is deemed to be a device.

provisions.label: Tobacco products
provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: This Act does not apply to a tobacco product as defined in section 2 of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act.

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: Despite the definition drug in section 2, this Act does not apply to a vaping product as defined in section 2 of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act by reason that it contains nicotine, unless the vaping product is manufactured, sold or represented for use in the diagnosis, treatment, mitigation or prevention of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or its symptoms, in human beings.

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: Despite the definition device in section 2, this Act does not apply to a vaping product as defined in section 2 of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act by reason that it is manufactured, sold or represented for use with a substance or mixture of substances that contains nicotine, unless the vaping product is manufactured, sold or represented for use in the diagnosis, treatment, mitigation or prevention of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or its symptoms, in human beings.

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: If the Minister believes that a thing falls within two or more of the definitions food, drug, cosmetic and device in section 2, the Minister may, by order, add a description of the thing to a single part of Schedule A that corresponds to one of the definitions in which the thing is believed to fall.

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: If the Minister believes that every thing in a class of things falls within the same two or more definitions food, drug, cosmetic and device in section 2, the Minister may, by order, add a description of the class to a single part of Schedule A that corresponds to one of the definitions in which the things in the class are believed to fall.

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: Before adding a description of a thing or a class of things to a part of Schedule A, the Minister shall consider the following factors:

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: Part 1 of Schedule A falls within the definition food in section 2 and does not fall within any of the other definitions referred to in subsections (1) and (2);

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: Part 2 of Schedule A falls within the definition drug in section 2 and does not fall within any of the other definitions referred to in subsections (1) and (2);

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: Part 3 of Schedule A falls within the definition cosmetic in section 2 and does not fall within any of the other definitions referred to in subsections (1) and (2); and

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.text: Part 4 of Schedule A falls within the definition device in section 2 and does not fall within any of the other definitions referred to in subsections (1) and (2).

provisions.heading: Interpretation and Application
provisions.marginalNote: Interpretation — definitions food, drug, cosmetic and device
provisions.text: For greater certainty, the definitions food, drug, cosmetic and device in section 2 are subject to subsection (4).

provisions.heading: General
provisions.text: No person shall advertise any food, drug, cosmetic or device to the general public as a treatment, preventative or cure for any of the diseases, disorders or abnormal physical states referred to in Schedule A.1.

provisions.heading: General
provisions.text: it is represented by label as a treatment, preventative or cure for any of the diseases, disorders or abnormal physical states referred to in Schedule A.1; or

provisions.heading: General
provisions.text: the person advertises it to the general public as a treatment, preventative or cure for any of the diseases, disorders or abnormal physical states referred to in Schedule A.1.

provisions.heading: General
provisions.text: No person shall sell any food, drug, cosmetic or device if

provisions.heading: General
provisions.marginalNote: Unauthorized advertising of contraceptive device prohibited
provisions.text: Except as authorized by regulation, no person shall advertise to the general public any contraceptive device or any drug manufactured, sold or represented for use in the prevention of conception.

provisions.label: Prohibition — clinical trials
provisions.heading: General
provisions.text: No person shall conduct a clinical trial in respect of a drug, device or prescribed food for a special dietary purpose unless the person holds an authorization issued under the regulations that authorizes the conduct of the clinical trial.

provisions.label: Terms and conditions — clinical trial authorizations
provisions.heading: General
provisions.marginalNote: Terms and conditions — clinical trial authorizations
provisions.text: The holder of an authorization referred to in section 3.1 shall comply with any terms and conditions of the authorization that are imposed by the Minister under regulations made under paragraph 30(1)(b.3).

provisions.label: Duty to publicize clinical trial information
provisions.heading: General
provisions.marginalNote: Duty to publicize clinical trial information
provisions.text: The holder of an authorization referred to in section 3.1 shall ensure that prescribed information concerning the clinical trial is made public within the prescribed time and in the prescribed manner.

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.text: A food does not have a poisonous or harmful substance in or on it for the purposes of paragraph (1)(a) — or is not adulterated for the purposes of paragraph (1)(d) — by reason only that it has in or on it a pest control product as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Pest Control Products Act, or any of its components or derivatives, if the amount of the pest control product or the components or derivatives in or on the food being sold does not exceed the maximum residue limit specified under section 9 or 10 of that Act.

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.text: No person shall label, package, treat, process, sell or advertise any food in a manner that is false, misleading or deceptive or is likely to create an erroneous impression regarding its character, value, quantity, composition, merit or safety.

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.marginalNote: Food labelled or packaged in contravention of regulations
provisions.text: An article of food that is not labelled or packaged as required by, or is labelled or packaged contrary to, the regulations shall be deemed to be labelled or packaged contrary to subsection (1).

provisions.heading: Food

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.text: Where a standard for a food has been prescribed, no person shall any article that is intended for sale and that is likely to be mistaken for that food unless the article complies with the prescribed standard.

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.text: Paragraphs (1)(b) and (c) do not apply to an operator of a conveyance that is used to carry an article or to a carrier of an article whose sole concern, in respect of the article, is the conveyance of the article unless the operator or carrier could, with reasonable diligence, have ascertained that the conveying or receiving for conveyance of the article or the possession of the article for the purpose of conveyance would be in contravention of subsection (1).

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.text: has been imported into Canada,

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.text: has been sent or conveyed from one province to another, or

provisions.heading: Food

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.marginalNote: Labelling, etc., of food that is imported or moved interprovincially
provisions.text: Where a standard for a food has been prescribed, no person shall label, package, sell or advertise any article that in such a manner that it is likely to be mistaken for that food unless the article complies with the prescribed standard.

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.text: The Governor in Council may, by regulation, identify a standard prescribed for a food, or any portion of the standard, as being necessary to prevent injury to the health of the consumer or purchaser of the food.

provisions.heading: Food
provisions.marginalNote: Where standard or portion thereof is identified
provisions.text: Where a standard or any portion of a standard prescribed for a food is identified by the Governor in Council pursuant to subsection (1), no person shall label, package, sell or advertise any article in such a manner that it is likely to be mistaken for that food unless the article complies with the standard or portion of a standard so identified.

provisions.label: Unsanitary manufacture, etc., of food
provisions.heading: Food
provisions.text: No person shall manufacture, prepare, preserve, package or store for sale any food under unsanitary conditions.

provisions.heading: Drugs
provisions.marginalNote: Drugs labelled or packaged in contravention of regulations
provisions.text: A drug that is not labelled or packaged as required by, or is labelled or packaged contrary to, the regulations shall be deemed to be labelled or packaged contrary to subsection (1).

provisions.heading: Drugs
provisions.text: Where a standard has been prescribed for a drug, no person shall label, package, sell or advertise any substance in such a manner that it is likely to be mistaken for that drug, unless the substance complies with the prescribed standard.

provisions.heading: Drugs
provisions.text: Where a standard has not been prescribed for a drug, but a standard for the drug is contained in any publication referred to in Schedule B, no person shall label, package, sell or advertise any substance in such a manner that it is likely to be mistaken for that drug, unless the substance complies with the standard.

provisions.label: Unsanitary manufacture, etc., of drug
provisions.heading: Drugs
provisions.text: No person shall manufacture, prepare, preserve, package or store for sale any drug under unsanitary conditions.

provisions.label: Drugs not to be sold unless safe manufacture indicated
provisions.heading: Drugs
provisions.marginalNote: Drugs not to be sold unless safe manufacture indicated
provisions.text: No person shall sell any drug described in Schedule C or D unless the Minister has, in prescribed form and manner, indicated that the premises in which the drug was manufactured and the process and conditions of manufacture therein are suitable to ensure that the drug will not be unsafe for use.

provisions.label: Drugs not to be sold unless safe batch indicated
provisions.heading: Drugs
provisions.marginalNote: Drugs not to be sold unless safe batch indicated
provisions.text: No person shall sell any drug described in Schedule E unless the Minister has, in prescribed form and manner, indicated that the batch from which the drug was taken is not unsafe for use.

provisions.label: Samples
provisions.heading: Drugs
provisions.text: No person shall distribute or cause to be distributed any drug as a sample except in accordance with the regulations.

provisions.label: Schedule F drugs not to be sold
provisions.heading: Drugs
provisions.marginalNote: Schedule F drugs not to be sold
provisions.text: No person shall sell any drug described in Schedule F.

provisions.label: Prohibition — animal testing
provisions.heading: Cosmetics
provisions.text: No person shall conduct a test on an animal that could cause pain, suffering or injury, whether physical or mental, to the animal if the purpose of the test is to meet, with respect to a cosmetic, a requirement under a provision of this Act or the regulations or to meet a requirement that relates to the safety of cosmetics under the law that applies in a foreign state.

provisions.heading: Cosmetics
provisions.text: No person shall make a claim on the label of or in an advertisement for a cosmetic that is likely to create an impression that the cosmetic was not tested on animals after the day on which this section comes into force unless the person has evidence that no such testing occurred after that day.

provisions.heading: Cosmetics
provisions.text: A person who makes a claim described in subsection (1) shall, on the request of the Minister, provide the Minister with the evidence referred to in that subsection.

provisions.label: Where standard prescribed for cosmetic
provisions.heading: Cosmetics
provisions.text: Where a standard has been prescribed for a cosmetic, no person shall label, package, sell or advertise any article in such a manner that it is likely to be mistaken for that cosmetic, unless the article complies with the prescribed standard.

provisions.label: Unsanitary conditions
provisions.heading: Cosmetics
provisions.text: No person shall manufacture, prepare, preserve, package or store for sale any cosmetic under unsanitary conditions.

provisions.label: Prohibited sales of devices
provisions.heading: Devices
provisions.text: No person shall sell any device that, when used according to directions or under such conditions as are customary or usual, may cause injury to the health of the purchaser or user thereof.

provisions.heading: Devices
provisions.text: No person shall label, package, treat, process, sell or advertise any device in a manner that is false, misleading or deceptive or is likely to create an erroneous impression regarding its design, construction, performance, intended use, quantity, character, value, composition, merit or safety.

provisions.heading: Devices
provisions.marginalNote: Devices labelled or packaged in contravention of regulations
provisions.text: A device that is not labelled or packaged as required by, or is labelled or packaged contrary to, the regulations shall be deemed to be labelled or packaged contrary to subsection (1).

provisions.label: Where standard prescribed for device
provisions.heading: Devices
provisions.text: Where a standard has been prescribed for a device, no person shall label, package, sell or advertise any article in such a manner that it is likely to be mistaken for that device, unless the article complies with the prescribed standard.

provisions.heading: Therapeutic Products
provisions.text: If the Minister believes that a therapeutic product may present a serious risk of injury to human health, the Minister may order a person to provide the Minister with information that is in the person’s control and that the Minister believes is necessary to determine whether the product presents such a risk.

provisions.heading: Therapeutic Products
provisions.text: The Minister may disclose confidential business information about a therapeutic prod­uct without notifying the person to whose business or affairs the information relates or obtaining their consent, if the Minister believes that the product may present a serious risk of injury to human health.

provisions.heading: Inspection, Seizure and Forfeiture
provisions.text: For the purposes of the administration and enforcement of this Act, the Minister may designate individuals or classes of individuals as inspectors to exercise powers or perform duties or functions in relation to any matter referred to in the designation.

provisions.heading: Inspection, Seizure and Forfeiture

provisions.text: If an inspector has seized an article under this Part and its owner or the person having possession, care or control of it at the time of its seizure consents to its forfeiture, the article is forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada and may be disposed of as the Minister or the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food may direct, at the expense of its owner or the person having possession, care or control of the article at the time of its seizure.

provisions.heading: Inspection, Seizure and Forfeiture
provisions.text: If a person has been convicted of a contravention of this Act or the regulations, the court or judge may, in addition to any punishment imposed, order that any article by means of or in relation to which the offence was committed, and any thing of a similar nature belonging to or in the possession, care or control of the person or found with the article, be forfeited. On the making of the order, the article and thing are forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada and may be disposed of as the Minister or the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food may direct, at the expense of the person who has been convicted.

provisions.heading: Inspection, Seizure and Forfeiture

provisions.text: Without prejudice to subsection (2), a judge of a superior court of the province in which any article is seized under this Part may, on the application of an inspector and on any notice to those persons that the judge directs, order that the article and any thing of a similar nature found with it be forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada, if the judge finds, after making any inquiry that the judge considers necessary, that the article is one by means of or in relation to which a provision of this Act or the regulations has been contravened. On the making of the order, the article or thing may be disposed of as the Minister or the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food may direct, at the expense of the owner of the article or the person having possession, care or control of it at the time of its seizure.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: An inspector who has reasonable grounds to believe that an imported food, drug, cosmetic or device does not meet the requirements of the regulations or was imported in contravention of a provision of this Act or the regulations may decide whether to give the owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of the food, drug, cosmetic or device, the opportunity to take a measure in respect of it.

provisions.text: If the inspector decides under subsection (1) not to give the owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of the food, drug, cosmetic or device the opportunity to take a measure in respect of it, the inspector shall exercise, in respect of the food, drug, cosmetic or device, any of the powers conferred by the provisions of this Act, other than this section, or of the regulations.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.marginalNote: Measures that may be taken and notice
provisions.text: However, if the inspector decides under subsection (1) to give the owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of the food, drug, cosmetic or device the opportunity to take a measure in respect of it, the inspector, or any other inspector who is informed of the decision, shall decide whether the owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of it may remove it from Canada at their expense, consent to its forfeiture or take either of these measures, and shall notify or cause to be notified the owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of the food, drug, cosmetic or device that they may take that measure within the period specified by the inspector or other inspector, as the case may be.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: If a person is notified under subsection (4) that they may consent to the forfeiture of the food, drug, cosmetic or device and the person consents to its forfeiture, the food, drug, cosmetic or device is forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada and may be disposed of, as the Minister may direct, at the person’s expense.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: An inspector who has reasonable grounds to believe that an imported food, drug, cosmetic or device does not meet the requirements of the regulations or was imported in contravention of a provision of this Act or the regulations may, by notice, whether the food, drug, cosmetic or device is seized or not, order its owner or importer, or the person having possession, care or control of it, to remove it from Canada at their expense or, if removal is not possible, to destroy it at their expense.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: The notice must be delivered personally to the owner or importer of the food, drug, cosmetic or device, or the person having possession, care or control of it, or sent to the owner, importer or person by any method that provides proof of delivery or by any prescribed method.

provisions.heading: Removal, Forfeiture or Destruction of Unlawful Imports
provisions.text: If the food, drug, cosmetic or device is not removed from Canada, or destroyed, within the period specified in the notice — or, if no period is specified, within 90 days after the day on which the notice was delivered or sent — it is, despite section 26, forfeited to Her Majesty in right of Canada and may be disposed of, as the Minister may direct, at the expense of the person to whom the notice was delivered or sent.

provisions.text: If the Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that a person has contravened, or is likely to contravene, this Act or the regulations, the Minister may order the person to take any measures that the Minister considers necessary to remedy the contravention or prevent it.

provisions.text: Without limiting the power conferred by any other subsection of this section, the Governor in Council may make any regulations that the Governor in Council considers necessary for the purpose of preventing shortages of therapeutic products or foods for a special dietary purpose in Canada or alleviating those shortages or their effects, in order to protect human health.

provisions.text: Without limiting the power conferred by any other subsection of this section, the Governor in Council may make any regulations that the Governor in Council considers necessary for the purpose of preventing, in respect of cosmetics, the conduct of a test on an animal that could cause pain, suffering or injury, whether physical or mental, to the animal, or preventing the purchaser or consumer of a cosmetic from being deceived or misled in respect of whether the cosmetic was tested on animals.

provisions.heading: Regulations
provisions.text: General Council means the General Council of the WTO established by paragraph 2 of Article IV of the Agreement Establishing the World Trade Organization, signed at Marrakesh on April 15, 1994. (Conseil général)

provisions.text: Subject to any regulations made under paragraph 30(1)(j.1) and if the Minister believes on reasonable grounds that the use of a therapeutic product, other than the intended use, may present a risk of injury to health, the Minister may, by order, establish rules in respect of the importation, sale, conditions of sale, advertising, manufacture, preparation, preservation, packaging, labelling, storage or testing of the therapeutic product for the purpose of preventing, managing or controlling the risk of injury to health.

provisions.heading: Supplementary Rules
provisions.text: For greater certainty, the Minister may, in the order, establish rules for the purpose of preventing the therapeutic product from being promoted for a use, other than the intended use, of a therapeutic product or preventing a use, other than the intended use, of a therapeutic product from being appealing.

provisions.heading: Supplementary Rules
provisions.text: The Minister may make the order despite any uncertainty respecting the risk of injury to health that the use of the therapeutic product, other than the intended use, may present.

provisions.heading: Supplementary Rules
provisions.text: Subject to any regulations made under paragraph 30(1)(j.1) and if the Minister believes on reasonable grounds that the use of a drug intended for an animal of a particular species, including a use other than the intended use, may present a risk of adverse effects to human beings, animals of a different species or the environment, the Minister may, by order, establish rules in respect of the importation, sale, conditions of sale, advertising, manufacture, preparation, preservation, packaging, labelling, storage or testing of the drug for the purpose of preventing, managing or controlling the risk of adverse effects.

provisions.heading: Supplementary Rules

provisions.text: A regulation made under this Act with respect to a food or therapeutic product and an order made under subsection 30.63(1) may incorporate by reference any document, regardless of its source, either as it exists on a particular date or as it is amended from time to time.

provisions.heading: Incorporation by Reference
provisions.text: The Minister shall ensure that any document that is incorporated by reference is accessible.

provisions.heading: Incorporation by Reference
provisions.text: A person is not liable to be found guilty of an offence for any contravention in respect of which a document that is incorporated by reference is relevant unless, at the time of the alleged contravention, the document was accessible as required by subsection (2) or it was otherwise accessible to the person.

provisions.heading: Incorporation by Reference
provisions.text: For greater certainty, a document that is incorporated by reference is not required to be transmitted for registration or published in the Canada Gazette by reason only that it is incorporated by reference.

provisions.label: Existing power not limited
provisions.heading: Incorporation by Reference
provisions.text: For greater certainty, an express power in this Act to incorporate a document by reference does not limit the power that otherwise exists to incorporate a document by reference in a regulation made under this Act.

provisions.heading: Fees

provisions.heading: Fees

provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: fees to be paid in respect of products, rights and privileges provided under this Act.

provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: The Minister may, by order, fix the following fees in relation to a drug, device, food or cosmetic:

provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: A fee fixed under paragraph (1)(a) may not exceed the cost to Her Majesty in right of Canada of providing the service or the use of the facility.

provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.marginalNote: Aggregate amount not to exceed cost
provisions.text: Fees fixed under paragraph (1)(b) may not in the aggregate exceed the cost to Her Majesty in right of Canada in respect of providing the regulatory processes or approvals.

provisions.label: Consultation
provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: Before making an order under subsection 30.61(1), the Minister shall consult with any persons that the Minister considers to be interested in the matter.

provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: The Minister may, by order, remit all or part of any fee fixed under subsection 30.61(1) and the interest on it.

provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: A remission granted under subsection (1) may be conditional.

provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: If a remission granted under subsection (1) is conditional and the condition is not fulfilled, then the remission is cancelled and is deemed never to have been granted.

provisions.label: Non-payment of fees
provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: The Minister may withdraw or withhold a service, the use of a facility, a regulatory process or approval or a product, right or privilege under this Act from any person who fails to pay the fee fixed for it under subsection 30.61(1).

provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: An order made under subsection 30.61(1) may prescribe rules for the adjustment of the amount of the fee by any amounts or ratios that are referred to in the order, for the period that is specified in the order.

provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: The amount of a fee that is subject to an adjustment rule remains unadjusted for the specified period unless, before the beginning of that period, the Minister publishes a notice in the Canada Gazette that specifies the adjusted amount and the manner in which it was determined.

provisions.label: Service Fees Act
provisions.heading: Fees
provisions.text: The Service Fees Act does not apply to a fee fixed under subsection 30.61(1).

provisions.heading: Costs
provisions.text: Her Majesty in right of Canada may recover, as a debt due to Her Majesty in right of Canada, any costs incurred by Her Majesty in right of Canada in relation to anything required or authorized under this Act, including the inspection of a place or the analysis, examination, storage, movement, seizure, detention, forfeiture, disposal or release of an article.

provisions.heading: Costs
provisions.text: Proceedings to recover a debt due to Her Majesty in right of Canada under subsection (1) shall not be commenced later than five years after the debt became payable.

provisions.heading: Costs
provisions.text: Any debt that may be recovered under subsection 30.7(1) in respect of which there is a default of payment, or the part of any such debt that has not been paid, may be certified by the Minister.

provisions.heading: Costs
provisions.text: On production to the Federal Court, a certificate made under subsection (1) shall be registered in that Court and, when registered, has the same force and effect, and all proceedings may be taken on the certificate, as if it were a judgment obtained in that Court for a debt of the amount specified in the certificate and all reasonable costs and charges attendant in the registration of the certificate.

provisions.label: Sentencing considerations
provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: A court that imposes a sentence for an offence under section 31.2 or 31.4 shall take into account, in addition to any other principles that it is required to consider, the following factors:

provisions.label: Parties to offence
provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: If a person other than an individual commits an offence under section 31.2, or commits an offence under section 31.4 by reason of contravening section 21.6, then any of the person’s directors, officers or agents or mandataries who directs, authorizes, assents to or acquiesces or participates in the commission of the offence is a party to the offence and is liable on conviction to the punishment provided for by this Act, even if the person is not prosecuted for the offence.

provisions.label: Continuing offence
provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: If an offence under section 31.2 or 31.4 is committed or continued on more than one day, it constitutes a separate offence for each day on which it is committed or continued.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: A prosecution for a summary conviction offence under this Act may be instituted at any time within two years after the time the subject-matter of the prosecution becomes known to the Minister or, in the case of a contravention of a provision of the Act that relates to food, to the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: A document purporting to have been issued by the Minister referred to in subsection (1), certifying the day on which the subject-matter of any prosecution became known to the Minister, is admissible in evidence without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed the document and is evidence of the matters asserted in it.

provisions.label: Venue
provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: A prosecution for a contravention of this Act or the regulations may be instituted, heard, tried or determined in the place in which the offence was committed or the subject-matter of the prosecution arose or in any place in which the accused is apprehended or happens to be.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.marginalNote: Notice of reliance on want of knowledge
provisions.text: Subsection (1) does not apply in any prosecution unless the accused, at least ten days before the day fixed for the trial, has given to the prosecutor notice in writing that the accused intends to avail himself of the provisions of subsection (1) and has disclosed to the prosecutor the name and address of the person from whom the accused purchased the article and the date of purchase.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: Subject to this section, in any prosecution for an offence under any of sections 31 to 31.2 and 31.4, a certificate purporting to be signed by an analyst and stating that an article, sample or substance has been submitted to, and analysed or examined by, the analyst and stating the results of the analysis or examination is admissible in evidence and, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, is proof of the statements contained in the certificate without proof of the signature or official character of the person appearing to have signed it.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: The party against whom a certificate of an analyst is produced pursuant to subsection (1) may, with leave of the court, require the attendance of the analyst for the purposes of cross-examination.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.marginalNote: Notice of intention to produce certificate
provisions.text: No certificate shall be admitted in evidence pursuant to subsection (1) unless, before the trial, the party intending to produce the certificate has given reasonable notice of that intention, together with a copy of the certificate, to the party against whom it is intended to be produced.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: For the purposes of this Act, service of any certificate referred to in subsection (1) may be proved by oral evidence given under oath by, or by the affidavit or solemn declaration of, the person claiming to have served it.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: Notwithstanding subsection (4), the court may require the person who appears to have signed an affidavit or solemn declaration referred to in that subsection to appear before it for examination or cross-examination in respect of the issue of proof of service.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: In a prosecution for a contravention of this Act or of the regulations made under this Part, proof that a package containing any article to which this Act or the regulations apply bore a name or address purporting to be the name or address of the person by whom it was manufactured or packaged is, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, proof that the article was manufactured or packaged, as the case may be, by the person whose name or address appeared on the package.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: In a prosecution for a contravention described in subsection (1), it is sufficient proof of the offence to establish that it was committed by an employee or agent of the accused whether or not the employee or agent is identified or has been prosecuted for the offence.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: In a prosecution for a contravention described in subsection (1), a copy of or extract from a document, including electronic data, that is certified to be a true copy by the inspector who made or took it under paragraph 23(2)(c), (d) or (f), as the case may be, is admissible in evidence and is, in the absence of evidence to the contrary, proof of its contents.

provisions.heading: Offences and Punishment
provisions.text: Where a person is prosecuted under this Part for having manufactured an adulterated food or drug for sale, and it is established that the person had in his possession or on his premises any substance the addition of which to that food or drug has been declared by regulation to cause the adulteration of the food or drug, the onus of proving that the food or drug was not adulterated by the addition of that substance lies on the accused.

sections.label: Short title
sections.text: Short title This Act may be cited as the Food and Drugs Act.

sections.label: “advanced therapeutic product”
sections.text: “advanced therapeutic product” advanced therapeutic product means a therapeutic product that is described in Schedule G or that belongs to a class of therapeutic products that is described in that Schedule; (produit thérapeutique innovant)

sections.label: “advertisement”
sections.text: “advertisement” advertisement includes any representation by any means whatever for the purpose of promoting directly or indirectly the sale or disposal of any food, drug, cosmetic or device; (publicité ou annonce)

sections.label: “analyst”
sections.text: “analyst” analyst means an individual designated as an analyst for the purposes of this Act under section 28 or under section 13 of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act; (analyste)

sections.label: “clinical trial”
sections.text: “clinical trial” clinical trial means a study, involving human subjects, for the purpose of discovering or verifying the effects of a drug, a device or a food for a special dietary purpose; (essai clinique)

sections.label: 2“confidential business information”
sections.text: 2“confidential business information” that is not publicly available, 2“confidential business information” in respect of which the person has taken measures that are reasonable in the circumstances to ensure that it remains not publicly available, and 2“confidential business information” that has actual or potential economic value to the person or their competitors because it is not publicly available and its disclosure would result in a material financial loss to the person or a material financial gain to their competitors; (renseignements commerciaux confidentiels)

sections.label: “confidential business information”
sections.text: “confidential business information” confidential business information, in respect of a person to whose business or affairs the information relates, means — subject to the regulations — business information

sections.label: “contraceptive device”
sections.text: “contraceptive device” contraceptive device means any instrument, apparatus, contrivance or substance other than a drug, that is manufactured, sold or represented for use in the prevention of conception; (moyen anticonceptionnel)

sections.label: “cosmetic”
sections.text: “cosmetic” cosmetic includes any substance or mixture of substances manufactured, sold or represented for use in cleansing, improving or altering the complexion, skin, hair or teeth, and includes deodorants and perfumes; (cosmétique)

sections.label: “Department”
sections.text: “Department” Department means the Department of Health; (ministère)

sections.label: 2“device”
sections.text: 2“device” diagnosing, treating, mitigating or preventing a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or any of their symptoms, in human beings or animals, 2“device” restoring, modifying or correcting the body structure of human beings or animals or the functioning of any part of the bodies of human beings or animals, 2“device” diagnosing pregnancy in human beings or animals, 2“device” caring for human beings or animals during pregnancy or at or after the birth of the offspring, including caring for the offspring, or 2“device” preventing conception in human beings or animals;

sections.label: “device”
sections.text: “device” device means an instrument, apparatus, contrivance or other similar article, or an in vitro reagent, including a component, part or accessory of any of them, that is manufactured, sold or represented for use in however, it does not include such an instrument, apparatus, contrivance or article, or a component, part or accessory of any of them, that does any of the actions referred to in paragraphs (a) to (e) solely by pharmacological, immunological or metabolic means or solely by chemical means in or on the body of a human being or animal; (instrument)

sections.label: 2“drug”
sections.text: 2“drug” the diagnosis, treatment, mitigation or prevention of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or its symptoms, in human beings or animals, 2“drug” restoring, correcting or modifying organic functions in human beings or animals, or 2“drug” disinfection in premises in which food is manufactured, prepared or kept; (drogue)

sections.label: “drug”
sections.text: “drug” drug includes any substance or mixture of substances manufactured, sold or represented for use in

sections.label: “food”
sections.text: “food” food includes any article manufactured, sold or represented for use as food or drink for human beings, chewing gum, and any ingredient that may be mixed with food for any purpose whatever; (aliment)

sections.label: 2“food for a special dietary purpose”
sections.text: 2“food for a special dietary purpose” to meet the particular requirements of an individual in whom a physical or physiological condition exists as a result of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or 2“food for a special dietary purpose” to be the sole or primary source of nutrition for an individual; (aliment à des fins diététiques spéciales)

sections.label: “food for a special dietary purpose”
sections.text: “food for a special dietary purpose” food for a special dietary purpose means a food that has been specially processed or formulated

sections.label: “foreign regulatory authority”
sections.text: “foreign regulatory authority” foreign regulatory authority means a government agency or other entity outside Canada that controls the manufacture, use or sale of therapeutic products or foods within its jurisdiction; (autorité réglementaire étrangère)

sections.label: “inspector”
sections.text: “inspector” inspector means an individual designated as an inspector for the purposes of this Act under subsection 22(1) or under section 13 of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act; (inspecteur)

sections.label: “label”
sections.text: “label” label includes any legend, word or mark attached to, included in, belonging to or accompanying any food, drug, cosmetic, device or package; (étiquette)

sections.label: “Minister”
sections.text: “Minister” Minister means the Minister of Health; (ministre)

sections.label: “package”
sections.text: “package” package includes any thing in which any food, drug, cosmetic or device is wholly or partly contained, placed or packed; (emballage)

sections.label: “person”
sections.text: “person” person means an individual or an organization as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code; (personne)

sections.label: “prescribed”
sections.text: “prescribed” prescribed means prescribed by the regulations; (Version anglaise seulement)

sections.text: “unsanitary conditions” unsanitary conditions means such conditions or circumstances as might contaminate with dirt or filth, or render injurious to health, a food, drug or cosmetic. (conditions non hygiéniques)

sections.label: Definitions
sections.text: Definitions In this Act,

sections.label: Non-corrective contact lenses
sections.text: Non-corrective contact lenses For the purposes of this Act, a non-corrective contact lens is deemed to be a device.

sections.label: Tobacco products
sections.text: Tobacco products This Act does not apply to a tobacco product as defined in section 2 of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act.

2.3 Despite the definition drug in section 2, this Act does not apply to a vaping product as defined in section 2 of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act by reason that it contains nicotine, unless the vaping product is manufactured, sold or represented for use in the diagnosis, treatment, mitigation or prevention of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or its symptoms, in human beings. 2.3 Despite the definition device in section 2, this Act does not apply to a vaping product as defined in section 2 of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act by reason that it is manufactured, sold or represented for use with a substance or mixture of substances that contains nicotine, unless the vaping product is manufactured, sold or represented for use in the diagnosis, treatment, mitigation or prevention of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or its symptoms, in human beings.

On the making of the order, the article or thing may be disposed of as the Minister or the Minister of Agriculture and Agri-Food may direct, at the expense of the owner of the article or the person having possession, care or control of it at the time of its seizure.

sections.label: Deeming
sections.text: Deeming For the purposes of any provision of this Act, other than sections 30.01, 30.02, 30.05 and 30.06, any reference to regulations made under this Act is deemed to include orders made under section 30.01, 30.02, 30.05 or 30.06.

30.1 The Minister may make an interim order that contains any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under this Act if the Minister believes that immediate action is required to deal with a significant risk, direct or indirect, to health, safety or the environment. 30.1(2) 14 days after it is made, unless it is approved by the Governor in Council, 30.1(2) the day on which it is repealed, 30.1(2) the day on which a regulation made under this Act, that has the same effect as the interim order, comes into force, and 30.1(2) one year after the interim order is made or any shorter period that may be specified in the interim order. 30.1 An interim order has effect from the time that it is made but ceases to have effect on the earliest of 30.1 No person shall be convicted of an offence consisting of a contravention of an interim order that, at the time of the alleged contravention, had not been published in the Canada Gazette unless it is proved that, at the time of the alleged contravention, the person had been notified of the interim order or reasonable steps had been taken to bring the purport of the interim order to the notice of those persons likely to be affected by it. 30.1(4) is exempt from the application of sections 3, 5 and 11 of the Statutory Instruments Act; and 30.1(4) shall be published in the Canada Gazette within 23 days after it is made. 30.1 An interim order 30.1 For the purpose of any provision of this Act other than this section and section 30.05, any reference to regulations made under this Act is deemed to include interim orders, and any reference to a regulation made under a specified provision of this Act is deemed to include a reference to the portion of an interim order containing any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under the specified provision. 30.1 A copy of each interim order must be tabled in each House of Parliament within 15 days after it is made. 30.1 In order to comply with subsection (6), the interim order may be sent to the Clerk of the House if the House is not sitting.

30.5 A regulation made under this Act with respect to a food or therapeutic product and an order made under subsection 30.63(1) may incorporate by reference any document, regardless of its source, either as it exists on a particular date or as it is amended from time to time. 30.5 The Minister shall ensure that any document that is incorporated by reference is accessible. 30.5 A person is not liable to be found guilty of an offence for any contravention in respect of which a document that is incorporated by reference is relevant unless, at the time of the alleged contravention, the document was accessible as required by subsection (2) or it was otherwise accessible to the person. 30.5 For greater certainty, a document that is incorporated by reference is not required to be transmitted for registration or published in the Canada Gazette by reason only that it is incorporated by reference.

sections.label: Existing power not limited
sections.text: Existing power not limited For greater certainty, an express power in this Act to incorporate a document by reference does not limit the power that otherwise exists to incorporate a document by reference in a regulation made under this Act.

sections.label: Consultation
sections.text: Consultation Before making an order under subsection 30.61(1), the Minister shall consult with any persons that the Minister considers to be interested in the matter.

30.63 The Minister may, by order, remit all or part of any fee fixed under subsection 30.61(1) and the interest on it. 30.63 A remission granted under subsection (1) may be conditional. 30.63 If a remission granted under subsection (1) is conditional and the condition is not fulfilled, then the remission is cancelled and is deemed never to have been granted.

sections.label: Non-payment of fees
sections.text: Non-payment of fees The Minister may withdraw or withhold a service, the use of a facility, a regulatory process or approval or a product, right or privilege under this Act from any person who fails to pay the fee fixed for it under subsection 30.61(1).

30.65 An order made under subsection 30.61(1) may prescribe rules for the adjustment of the amount of the fee by any amounts or ratios that are referred to in the order, for the period that is specified in the order. 30.65 The amount of a fee that is subject to an adjustment rule remains unadjusted for the specified period unless, before the beginning of that period, the Minister publishes a notice in the Canada Gazette that specifies the adjusted amount and the manner in which it was determined.

sections.label: Service Fees Act
sections.text: Service Fees Act The Service Fees Act does not apply to a fee fixed under subsection 30.61(1).

30.7 Her Majesty in right of Canada may recover, as a debt due to Her Majesty in right of Canada, any costs incurred by Her Majesty in right of Canada in relation to anything required or authorized under this Act, including the inspection of a place or the analysis, examination, storage, movement, seizure, detention, forfeiture, disposal or release of an article. 30.7 Proceedings to recover a debt due to Her Majesty in right of Canada under subsection (1) shall not be commenced later than five years after the debt became payable.

30.8 Any debt that may be recovered under subsection 30.7(1) in respect of which there is a default of payment, or the part of any such debt that has not been paid, may be certified by the Minister. 30.8 On production to the Federal Court, a certificate made under subsection (1) shall be registered in that Court and, when registered, has the same force and effect, and all proceedings may be taken on the certificate, as if it were a judgment obtained in that Court for a debt of the amount specified in the certificate and all reasonable costs and charges attendant in the registration of the certificate.

sections.label: Parties to offence
sections.text: Parties to offence If a person other than an individual commits an offence under section 31.2, or commits an offence under section 31.4 by reason of contravening section 21.6, then any of the person’s directors, officers or agents or mandataries who directs, authorizes, assents to or acquiesces or participates in the commission of the offence is a party to the offence and is liable on conviction to the punishment provided for by this Act, even if the person is not prosecuted for the offence.

sections.label: Continuing offence
sections.text: Continuing offence If an offence under section 31.2 or 31.4 is committed or continued on more than one day, it constitutes a separate offence for each day on which it is committed or continued.

sections.label: Venue
sections.text: Venue A prosecution for a contravention of this Act or the regulations may be instituted, heard, tried or determined in the place in which the offence was committed or the subject-matter of the prosecution arose or in any place in which the accused is apprehended or happens to be.

fullText:
Short title
Short title This Act may be cited as the Food and Drugs Act.

“advanced therapeutic product” advanced therapeutic product means a therapeutic product that is described in Schedule G or that belongs to a class of therapeutic products that is described in that Schedule; (produit thérapeutique innovant)

“advertisement” advertisement includes any representation by any means whatever for the purpose of promoting directly or indirectly the sale or disposal of any food, drug, cosmetic or device; (publicité ou annonce)

“analyst” analyst means an individual designated as an analyst for the purposes of this Act under section 28 or under section 13 of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act; (analyste)

“clinical trial” clinical trial means a study, involving human subjects, for the purpose of discovering or verifying the effects of a drug, a device or a food for a special dietary purpose; (essai clinique)

2“confidential business information” that is not publicly available,

2“confidential business information” in respect of which the person has taken measures that are reasonable in the circumstances to ensure that it remains not publicly available, and

2“confidential business information” that has actual or potential economic value to the person or their competitors because it is not publicly available and its disclosure would result in a material financial loss to the person or a material financial gain to their competitors; (renseignements commerciaux confidentiels)

“confidential business information” confidential business information, in respect of a person to whose business or affairs the information relates, means — subject to the regulations — business information

“contraceptive device” contraceptive device means any instrument, apparatus, contrivance or substance other than a drug, that is manufactured, sold or represented for use in the prevention of conception; (moyen anticonceptionnel)

“cosmetic” cosmetic includes any substance or mixture of substances manufactured, sold or represented for use in cleansing, improving or altering the complexion, skin, hair or teeth, and includes deodorants and perfumes; (cosmétique)

“Department” Department means the Department of Health; (ministère)

2“device” diagnosing, treating, mitigating or preventing a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or any of their symptoms, in human beings or animals,

2“device” restoring, modifying or correcting the body structure of human beings or animals or the functioning of any part of the bodies of human beings or animals,

2“device” diagnosing pregnancy in human beings or animals,

2“device” caring for human beings or animals during pregnancy or at or after the birth of the offspring, including caring for the offspring, or

2“device” preventing conception in human beings or animals;

“device” device means an instrument, apparatus, contrivance or other similar article, or an in vitro reagent, including a component, part or accessory of any of them, that is manufactured, sold or represented for use in however, it does not include such an instrument, apparatus, contrivance or article, or a component, part or accessory of any of them, that does any of the actions referred to in paragraphs (a) to (e) solely by pharmacological, immunological or metabolic means or solely by chemical means in or on the body of a human being or animal; (instrument)

2“drug” the diagnosis, treatment, mitigation or prevention of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or its symptoms, in human beings or animals,

2“drug” restoring, correcting or modifying organic functions in human beings or animals, or

2“drug” disinfection in premises in which food is manufactured, prepared or kept; (drogue)

“drug” drug includes any substance or mixture of substances manufactured, sold or represented for use in

“food” food includes any article manufactured, sold or represented for use as food or drink for human beings, chewing gum, and any ingredient that may be mixed with food for any purpose whatever; (aliment)

2“food for a special dietary purpose” to meet the particular requirements of an individual in whom a physical or physiological condition exists as a result of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or

2“food for a special dietary purpose” to be the sole or primary source of nutrition for an individual; (aliment à des fins diététiques spéciales)

“food for a special dietary purpose” food for a special dietary purpose means a food that has been specially processed or formulated

“foreign regulatory authority” foreign regulatory authority means a government agency or other entity outside Canada that controls the manufacture, use or sale of therapeutic products or foods within its jurisdiction; (autorité réglementaire étrangère)

“inspector” inspector means an individual designated as an inspector for the purposes of this Act under subsection 22(1) or under section 13 of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency Act; (inspecteur)

“label” label includes any legend, word or mark attached to, included in, belonging to or accompanying any food, drug, cosmetic, device or package; (étiquette)

“Minister” Minister means the Minister of Health; (ministre)

“package” package includes any thing in which any food, drug, cosmetic or device is wholly or partly contained, placed or packed; (emballage)

“person” person means an individual or an organization as defined in section 2 of the Criminal Code; (personne)

“prescribed” prescribed means prescribed by the regulations; (Version anglaise seulement)

2“sell” offer for sale, expose for sale or have in possession for sale — or distribute to one or more persons, whether or not the distribution is made for consideration, and

2“sell” lease, offer for lease, expose for lease or have in possession for lease; (vente)

“sell” sell includes

“therapeutic product” therapeutic product means a drug or device or any combination of drugs and devices; (produit thérapeutique)

2“therapeutic product authorization”(a) authorizes the conduct of a clinical trial in respect of a therapeutic product and is issued under the regulations, or

2“therapeutic product authorization”(a) authorizes, as the case may be, the import, sale, advertisement, manufacture, preparation, preservation, packaging, labelling, storage or testing of a therapeutic product and is issued under subsection 21.92(1) or the regulations, or

2“therapeutic product authorization” an authorization, including a licence, that

2“therapeutic product authorization” an authorization, including a licence, that would fall within paragraph (a) if the authorization were not suspended; (autorisation relative à un produit thérapeutique)

“therapeutic product authorization” therapeutic product authorization means

“unsanitary conditions” unsanitary conditions means such conditions or circumstances as might contaminate with dirt or filth, or render injurious to health, a food, drug or cosmetic. (conditions non hygiéniques)

Definitions In this Act,

Non-corrective contact lenses
Non-corrective contact lenses For the purposes of this Act, a non-corrective contact lens is deemed to be a device.

Tobacco products
Tobacco products This Act does not apply to a tobacco product as defined in section 2 of the Tobacco and Vaping Products Act.

Vaping products

Prohibition — clinical trials
Prohibition — clinical trials No person shall conduct a clinical trial in respect of a drug, device or prescribed food for a special dietary purpose unless the person holds an authorization issued under the regulations that authorizes the conduct of the clinical trial.

Terms and conditions — clinical trial authorizations
Terms and conditions — clinical trial authorizations The holder of an authorization referred to in section 3.1 shall comply with any terms and conditions of the authorization that are imposed by the Minister under regulations made under paragraph 30(1)(b.3).

Duty to publicize clinical trial information The holder of an authorization referred to in section 3.1 shall ensure that prescribed information concerning the clinical trial is made public within the prescribed time and in the prescribed manner.

Sentencing considerations
Sentencing considerations A court that imposes a sentence for an offence under section 31.2 or 31.4 shall take into account, in addition to any other principles that it is required to consider, the following factors:

Parties to offence
Parties to offence If a person other than an individual commits an offence under section 31.2, or commits an offence under section 31.4 by reason of contravening section 21.6, then any of the person’s directors, officers or agents or mandataries who directs, authorizes, assents to or acquiesces or participates in the commission of the offence is a party to the offence and is liable on conviction to the punishment provided for by this Act, even if the person is not prosecuted for the offence.

Continuing offence
Continuing offence If an offence under section 31.2 or 31.4 is committed or continued on more than one day, it constitutes a separate offence for each day on which it is committed or continued.
